name: memray (Python Memory Profiler)

performance.notes: Low overhead; C extension hooks into Python allocator

agent_readiness.notes: Python memory profiler from Bloomberg. python -m memray run script.py. memray flamegraph memray.bin. memray table memray.bin. memray stats memray.bin. Live mode: memray run --live script.py. Decorator: @memray.profile. Pytest plugin: pytest --memray. Tracks native extensions (C/C++). Reports: flamegraph, table, tree, stats, summary. Finds memory leaks, bloated allocations, retention chains. Best Python memory profiler available.

gotchas: Binary .bin output requires memray commands to read
Memory profiling adds overhead — not suitable for continuous production
Native extension memory tracked but symbolization may be incomplete
best_when: Understanding memory allocation patterns in data processing
pytest integration for memory regression testing
Finding OOM causes in data pipelines
summary: memray is Bloomberg's Python memory profiler — the best tool for Python memory analysis. python -m memray run script.py. memray flamegraph for allocation flame graphs. Tracks native C extension allocations. pytest plugin (--memray) for memory regression tests. Live mode for interactive exploration. Finds memory leaks and bloated allocations in pandas/numpy/etc. Linux primary, macOS limited.
